title: Dense Smoky Polish Semolina Pilaf
description: A hearty, smoky Polish dish featuring semolina flour, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences. This pilaf is dense, comforting, and full of flavor.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large wok or skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to caramelize.
Add the grated carrot and cook for another 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the sliced mushroom and cook until it releases its moisture and starts to brown.
Add the diced smoked tofu and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the semolina flour and stir to combine, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
